blocks.content: Not because she was done — the painting wasn't done — but because there was something she seemed to need to say while standing still.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: I need to tell you something.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: I know I'm not easy. To be around. Not in the way you'd think — I'm not dramatic, I don't create problems — but there's a version of me that comes with things attached. And I'm aware of that. And I don't want to—

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: I don't want you to get into something without knowing that.
He was quiet for a moment.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: You don't know what you mean yet. You don't know—
She caught herself. Something moved across her expression — close, and then carefully put away.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: No. But I know what I see.
She looked at him for a long moment. The apartment was very quiet. The rain was nearly gone.
She nodded, very small. Not agreement exactly — something closer to the word enough.
